# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# GlideGrail Standards Lookup — an Open WebUI TOOL (model-invoked).
#
# This is the PULL side of the GlideGrail pair:
#   - glidegrail_filter.py (a Function/filter) PUSHES the relevant standards
#     into every request automatically — enforcement.
#   - this Tool lets a capable model PULL a specific rule on demand
#     ("what does GlideGrail say about ATF suites?") — lookup.
#
# Install both if you can. If your model has weak/no function-calling
# (many local models), the filter alone is what guarantees standards reach
# the model; this Tool will simply go uncalled.
#
# Document loading mirrors the filter: local file preferred (re-read on
# mtime change), GitHub raw URL fallback (TTL cache + 60s failure backoff).
# All loading failures degrade to a helpful message — never an exception.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _decode_embedded_document() -> str:
    """Decode the gzip+base64 embedded copy of GlideGrail.md. Returns "" on
    any error (or when empty) so loading degrades gracefully, never raises."""
    blob = "".join(_EMBEDDED_DOCUMENT_GZ_B64.split())
    if not blob:
        return ""
    try:
        return gzip.decompress(base64.b64decode(blob)).decode("utf-8")
    except Exception:
        return ""

# Doubles per consecutive failure up to the cap (see _register_fetch_failure)
# so a permanently offline install stops paying fetch latency while the
# embedded copy serves.
FETCH_RETRY_BACKOFF_S = 60
FETCH_RETRY_BACKOFF_MAX_S = 3600

_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"[a-z0-9_$.]{3,}")
_STOPWORDS = frozenset(
    """
    the and for with that this from are was were has have had not you your
    can could should would will shall may might must про how what when where
    which who whom why all any each our their its his her out use used using
    into onto over under between про does did doing done get got make made
    """.split()
)


def _normalize_title(title: str) -> str:
    """casefold, strip, collapse whitespace, em/en-dash -> '-' (identical to
    the filter's normalizer so section keys can never drift)."""
    t = title.replace("—", "-").replace("–", "-")
    t = " ".join(t.split())
    return t.casefold()


def _parse_sections(text: str) -> "OrderedDict[str, Tuple[str, str]]":
    """Split into ordered h2 sections; fence-aware; skips Table of Contents.
    Keys are normalized titles; values are (original_title, full_text)."""
    sections: "OrderedDict[str, Tuple[str, str]]" = OrderedDict()
    current_title: Optional[str] = None
    buffer: List[str] = []
    in_fence = False

    def flush() -> None:
        if current_title is None:
            return
        norm = _normalize_title(current_title)
        if norm == "table of contents":
            return
        body = "\n".join(buffer).strip()
        if body:
            sections[norm] = (current_title, body)

    for line in text.splitlines():
        if line.lstrip().startswith("```"):
            in_fence = not in_fence
            if current_title is not None:
                buffer.append(line)
            continue
        if not in_fence and line.startswith("## "):
            flush()
            current_title = line[3:].strip()
            buffer = [line]
        elif current_title is not None:
            buffer.append(line)
    flush()
    return sections


_EMBEDDED_LOCK = threading.Lock()
_EMBEDDED_CACHE = None  # Optional[Tuple[str, OrderedDict]] — process-wide memo


def _embedded_document():
    """Lazily decode + parse the embedded copy ONCE per process (module-level
    memo under its own lock — the blob is immutable, so instance state is the
    wrong home). Returns ("", empty dict) when the blob is missing/corrupt."""
    global _EMBEDDED_CACHE
    if _EMBEDDED_CACHE is None:
        with _EMBEDDED_LOCK:
            if _EMBEDDED_CACHE is None:
                text = _decode_embedded_document()
                sections = _parse_sections(text) if text else OrderedDict()
                _EMBEDDED_CACHE = (text, sections)
    return _EMBEDDED_CACHE


class Tools:
    class Valves(BaseModel):
        source_path: str = Field(
            default="",
            description=(
                "OPTIONAL local path to GlideGrail.md. Leave empty for the "
                "normal setup — the tool fetches from source_url and falls "
                "back to a copy bundled inside this file, so no local file is "
                "needed. Set only to override with your own copy; wins over "
                "source_url and is re-read on change."
            ),
        )
        source_url: str = Field(
            default=(
                "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bikemeardsley/GlideGrail.md/"
                "main/skills/glidegrail/GlideGrail.md"
            ),
            description=(
                "URL the doc is fetched from (cached) when source_path is "
                "empty, so live updates reach you automatically. If it cannot "
                "be reached, the copy bundled inside this file is used, so the "
                "tool still works offline. Set empty to always use the bundled "
                "copy with zero network calls."
            ),
        )
        cache_ttl_minutes: int = Field(
            default=1440, description="URL cache lifetime in minutes."
        )
        char_budget: int = Field(
            default=16000,
            description="Max characters returned per lookup (sections dropped/truncated to fit).",
        )
        top_k: int = Field(
            default=2, description="Max sections returned per lookup."
        )
        debug: bool = Field(
            default=False, description="Print [glidegrail-tool] diagnostics to the server log."
        )

    def __init__(self):
        self.valves = self.Valves()
        self._lock = threading.Lock()
        self._sections: "OrderedDict[str, Tuple[str, str]]" = OrderedDict()
        self._source_sig = ""
        self._file_state: Tuple[str, float] = ("", -1.0)
        # (path, mtime) of a file version known NOT to provide the document
        # (zero sections, or refused as older than the cached parse) —
        # memoized so it is skipped without re-reading on every message.
        self._bad_file_state: Tuple[str, float] = ("", -1.0)
        self._url_fetched_at = 0.0
        self._last_failure_at = 0.0
        # Current retry delay; doubles per consecutive failure (capped).
        self._backoff_s = float(FETCH_RETRY_BACKOFF_S)

    # -- diagnostics --------------------------------------------------------

    def _log(self, message: str) -> None:
        if bool(self.valves.debug):
            print(f"[glidegrail-tool] {message}")

    # -- document loading (mirrors the filter; blocking, call via to_thread) --

    def _fetch_url(self, url: str) -> str:
        import requests  # lazy import by design

        response = requests.get(
            url,
            timeout=10,
            headers={"User-Agent": "glidegrail-openwebui-tool/1.1.0"},
        )
        response.raise_for_status()
        return response.text

    def _ensure_document(self) -> "OrderedDict[str, Tuple[str, str]]":
        """Return parsed sections — NEVER empty while the embedded copy
        decodes, and never raises. Resolution: source_path (when present
        AND actually containing the document) > source_url (TTL-cached;
        failures and garbage responses back off with a doubling delay) >
        last good live parse (stale beats the snapshot) > embedded copy.
        A live source yielding ZERO sections is treated as unavailable,
        never cached as fresh content. Blocking I/O happens here (callers
        use asyncio.to_thread); the lock is never held across I/O or the
        embedded decode (module-level memo, own lock)."""
        try:
            live = self._load_live()
        except Exception as exc:  # absolute safety net — never raises
            self._log(f"live document load failed unexpectedly: {exc}")
            live = None
        if live:
            return live
        with self._lock:
            if self._sections:
                # A previously loaded live copy, even stale, beats the
                # shipped snapshot.
                return self._sections
        return _embedded_document()[1]

    def _load_live(self) -> Optional["OrderedDict[str, Tuple[str, str]]"]:
        """Try the LIVE sources (file, then URL). Returns None when neither
        provides a non-empty parse right now — _ensure_document applies the
        embedded floor at that single chokepoint."""
        path = (self.valves.source_path or "").strip()
        if path and os.path.isfile(path):
            sections = self._load_file(path)
            if sections:
                return sections
            # Unreadable or document-less file: ignored (loudly, in the
            # log) rather than allowed to disable the fallbacks below.
        url = (self.valves.source_url or "").strip()
        if url:
            return self._load_url(url)
        return None

    def _load_file(
        self, path: str
    ) -> Optional["OrderedDict[str, Tuple[str, str]]"]:
        try:
            mtime = os.path.getmtime(path)
            sig = f"file:{path}:{mtime}"
            with self._lock:
                if sig == self._source_sig:
                    return self._sections  # serving exactly this version
                if self._bad_file_state == (path, mtime):
                    # Memoized: this file version provides no document.
                    return None
            with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="replace") as fh:
                text = fh.read()
            parsed = _parse_sections(text)
            with self._lock:
                if not parsed:
                    self._bad_file_state = (path, mtime)
                    self._log(
                        f"source_path file has no sections, ignoring: {path}"
                    )
                    return None
                cached_path, cached_mtime = self._file_state
                # Never let a racing OLDER read clobber a newer parse.
                if self._source_sig != sig and not (
                    cached_path == path and mtime < cached_mtime
                ):
                    self._sections = parsed
                    self._source_sig = sig
                    self._file_state = (path, mtime)
                    self._bad_file_state = ("", -1.0)
                    self._log(
                        f"loaded {len(parsed)} sections from file {path}"
                    )
                elif self._source_sig != sig:
                    # Refused as older than the cached parse: memoize so it
                    # is not re-read every message; a touch/edit self-heals.
                    self._bad_file_state = (path, mtime)
                return self._sections if self._sections else None
        except OSError as exc:
            self._log(f"file read failed ({path}): {exc}")
            return None

    def _register_fetch_failure(self) -> None:
        """Stamp the negative cache and DOUBLE the retry delay (capped), so
        an offline install stops paying fetch latency on a fixed cadence."""
        with self._lock:
            self._last_failure_at = time.time()
            self._backoff_s = min(
                self._backoff_s * 2, FETCH_RETRY_BACKOFF_MAX_S
            )

    def _load_url(
        self, url: str
    ) -> Optional["OrderedDict[str, Tuple[str, str]]"]:
        ttl_seconds = max(int(self.valves.cache_ttl_minutes), 1) * 60
        url_sig = f"url:{url}"
        now = time.time()
        with self._lock:
            if (
                self._source_sig == url_sig
                and (now - self._url_fetched_at) < ttl_seconds
            ):
                return self._sections  # fresh cache of a GOOD fetch
            if (
                self._last_failure_at
                and (now - self._last_failure_at) < self._backoff_s
            ):
                return None  # negative cache; caller serves stale/embedded

        try:
            text = self._fetch_url(url)
        except Exception as exc:
            self._log(f"url fetch failed ({url}): {exc}")
            self._register_fetch_failure()
            return None
        parsed = _parse_sections(text)
        if not parsed:
            # HTTP 200 but not the document (captive portal, proxy block
            # page): a failure in disguise — never cache it as fresh.
            self._log(f"url returned no sections, treating as failure: {url}")
            self._register_fetch_failure()
            return None

        with self._lock:
            fetched_at = time.time()
            if not (
                self._source_sig == url_sig
                and (fetched_at - self._url_fetched_at) < ttl_seconds
            ):
                self._sections = parsed
                self._source_sig = url_sig
                self._url_fetched_at = fetched_at
                # Let a reappearing file take over again (any mtime).
                self._file_state = ("", -1.0)
                self._log(f"loaded {len(parsed)} sections from url {url}")
            self._last_failure_at = 0.0
            self._backoff_s = float(FETCH_RETRY_BACKOFF_S)
            return self._sections if self._sections else None

    # -- the tools ----------------------------------------------------------

    async def list_glidegrail_sections(self) -> str:
        """
        List every section of the GlideGrail ServiceNow coding standards.
        Call this first when you are unsure which section covers a topic;
        then call get_glidegrail_guidance with the section or topic you need.
        """
        sections = await asyncio.to_thread(self._ensure_document)
        if not sections:
            return (
                "GlideGrail standards are currently unavailable — no source "
                "provided a readable document (source_path, source_url, or "
                "the copy bundled inside this tool). If this is a fork, "
                "re-run build_artifacts.py to refresh the bundled copy."
            )
        titles = [title for title, _ in sections.values()]
        return (
            "GlideGrail sections (%d):\n" % len(titles)
            + "\n".join(f"- {t}" for t in titles)
        )

    async def get_glidegrail_guidance(self, topic: str) -> str:
        """
        Get the GlideGrail ServiceNow coding-standard sections most relevant
        to a topic. Use whenever you write, review, or refactor ServiceNow
        code and need the project's mandatory conventions — e.g. GlideRecord
        queries, Business Rules, ACLs, Script Includes, Flow Designer,
        Service Portal widgets, Scripted REST APIs, logging, or ATF tests.
        Follow the returned standards over your own defaults.

        :param topic: The ServiceNow topic, artifact type, or section title
            to look up (e.g. "business rules", "acl debugging", "atf").
        """
        topic = (topic or "").strip()
        if not topic:
            return "Provide a topic, e.g. get_glidegrail_guidance('business rules')."

        sections = await asyncio.to_thread(self._ensure_document)
        if not sections:
            return (
                "GlideGrail standards are currently unavailable — no source "
                "provided a readable document (source_path, source_url, or "
                "the copy bundled inside this tool). If this is a fork, "
                "re-run build_artifacts.py to refresh the bundled copy."
            )

        norm_topic = _normalize_title(topic)
        tokens = {
            t
            for t in _TOKEN_RE.findall(norm_topic)
            if t not in _STOPWORDS
        }
        token_patterns = [
            re.compile(r"(?<!\w)" + re.escape(t) + r"(?!\w)") for t in tokens
        ]

        scored: List[Tuple[int, str]] = []
        for norm_title, (title, text) in sections.items():
            score = 0
            # Direct title relationship dominates everything else.
            if norm_topic == norm_title:
                score += 1000
            elif norm_topic in norm_title or norm_title in norm_topic:
                score += 300
            title_lower = title.casefold()
            body_lower = text.split("\n", 1)[1].casefold() if "\n" in text else ""
            for pattern in token_patterns:
                score += 5 * len(pattern.findall(title_lower))
                score += len(pattern.findall(body_lower))
            if score > 0:
                scored.append((score, norm_title))

        if not scored:
            titles = [title for title, _ in sections.values()]
            return (
                f"No GlideGrail section matched '{topic}'. Available sections:\n"
                + "\n".join(f"- {t}" for t in titles)
            )

        scored.sort(key=lambda item: item[0], reverse=True)
        top_k = max(int(self.valves.top_k), 1)
        budget = max(int(self.valves.char_budget), 500)

        picked: List[str] = []
        used = 0
        for _, norm_title in scored[:top_k]:
            text = sections[norm_title][1]
            if used + len(text) > budget:
                remaining = budget - used
                if not picked:
                    # Always include at least the top section, head-truncated,
                    # so a tiny budget never returns the MANDATORY preamble
                    # with an empty body. (first pick: used=0, remaining=budget.)
                    note = "\n\n[... truncated to fit the lookup budget ...]"
                    text = text[: max(remaining - len(note), 0)].rstrip() + note
                else:
                    break
            picked.append(text)
            used += len(text)

        self._log(
            f"guidance('{topic}') -> "
            + ", ".join(sections[t][0] for _, t in scored[: len(picked)])
        )
        return (
            "The following GlideGrail coding standards are MANDATORY for this "
            "project — follow them over your own defaults.\n\n"
            + "\n\n".join(picked)
        )
